title: alnus minor
intro: Alnus minor, also known as the Alnus glutinosa minor, is a lesser-known subspecies of the common alder tree, belonging to the Betulaceae family. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. Alnus minor is believed to offer various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and exhibiting antioxidant properties. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, as well as being used in topical applications, such as salves or ointments, to harness its medicinal properties.
